title: Arvoreen
summary: Halfling god of protection, vigilance, and war, the fierce defender who guards the halfling homeland.
content:
## Arvoreen

**Title:** The Defender, the Vigilant Guardian, the Wary Sword
**Alignment:** Lawful Good
**Domains:** War
**Symbol:** Two short swords crossed
**Pantheon:** Yondalla's Children

Arvoreen is the halfling god of protection, defensive combat, war, vigilance, and duty. He is the most martial of the halfling deities, a tireless guardian who watches over halfling communities with unwavering dedication. Unlike the typically easygoing nature of halfling culture, Arvoreen is stern, disciplined, and ever-alert for threats. He teaches that the peaceful lives halflings cherish can only be maintained through constant readiness and the willingness to fight in their defense.

Arvoreen's clergy form the backbone of halfling military forces, training local militias, organizing watches, and leading defense efforts when threats arise. They patrol the borders of halfling settlements and maintain networks of scouts and sentries. Temples to Arvoreen are practical fortifications disguised as community halls, stocked with weapons and provisions for times of crisis. His followers believe that vigilance is the price of peace, and that even the smallest warrior can turn back the greatest evil when defending home and hearth.

title: Brandobaris
summary: Halfling god of stealth, thievery, and adventure, the irrepressible rogue who lives for daring escapades.
content:
## Brandobaris

**Title:** Master of Stealth, the Irrepressible Scamp, the Friendly Rapscallion
**Alignment:** Neutral
**Domains:** Trickery
**Symbol:** A halfling footprint
**Pantheon:** Yondalla's Children

Brandobaris is the halfling god of stealth, thievery, and adventuring. He is the patron of halfling rogues, explorers, and anyone who lives by their wits and quick fingers. Charming, audacious, and perpetually in motion, Brandobaris embodies the halfling love of excitement and the thrill of a well-executed heist. His legendary exploits include stealing treasures from dragons, outwitting archdevils, and escaping from seemingly inescapable prisons, though the details change with every telling.

Brandobaris's clergy are wanderers, tricksters, and adventurers who refuse to be tied down by routine or convention. They serve as scouts, spies, and procurers of hard-to-find goods for their communities, always operating with a wink and a grin. Temples to Brandobaris are rare and never permanent; his worship takes place around campfires, in taverns, and wherever halflings gather to share tales of daring exploits. His followers believe that life is too short to spend in one place, and that the greatest treasures are the stories you collect along the way.

title: Cyrrollalee
summary: Halfling goddess of hearth, home, and hospitality, the warm-hearted keeper of the halfling way of life.
content:
## Cyrrollalee

**Title:** The Hand of Fellowship, the Hearthkeeper
**Alignment:** Lawful Good
**Domains:** Life
**Symbol:** An open door
**Pantheon:** Yondalla's Children

Cyrrollalee is the halfling goddess of hearth, home, friendship, trust, and hospitality. She embodies the warm, welcoming spirit that defines halfling culture at its finest. Every cozy burrow with a fire in the hearth and a meal on the table is a shrine to Cyrrollalee, and every act of genuine hospitality is an offering in her name. She teaches that the bonds of friendship and community are the greatest treasures any halfling can possess, worth more than all the gold in the world.

Cyrrollalee's clergy are community leaders, innkeepers, cooks, and counselors who work to strengthen the bonds that hold halfling settlements together. They organize feasts, mediate disputes, welcome strangers, and care for those in need. Temples to Cyrrollalee are warm, comfortable gathering places where all are welcome, often doubling as community kitchens and guest houses. Her followers open their doors to travelers without hesitation and believe that a stranger is simply a friend who has not yet been properly fed.

title: Sheela Peryroyl
summary: Halfling goddess of nature, weather, and agriculture, the green sister who nurtures the growing things of the world.
content:
## Sheela Peryroyl

**Title:** Green Sister, the Watchful Mother, the Green Children's Mother
**Alignment:** Neutral
**Domains:** Nature, Tempest
**Symbol:** A daisy
**Pantheon:** Yondalla's Children

Sheela Peryroyl is the halfling goddess of nature, agriculture, weather, song, dance, and beauty. She is the patron of halfling farmers, gardeners, and druids, and her influence is felt in every bountiful harvest, gentle rain, and wildflower meadow. Sheela balances the wild unpredictability of nature with the careful cultivation of the land, teaching her followers to work with the natural world rather than against it. Her dual nature encompasses both the nurturing warmth of a sunny day and the cleansing fury of a thunderstorm.

Sheela's clergy are druids, herbalists, and farmers who tend the lands surrounding halfling communities with skill and devotion. They read weather patterns, bless crops, protect natural spaces from despoilation, and lead celebrations tied to the agricultural calendar. Temples to Sheela are outdoor shrines set in gardens, orchards, or meadows, decorated with garlands of wildflowers. Her followers dance barefoot in spring rains, sing to encourage growing things, and believe that the health of the land reflects the health of the community that tends it.

title: Urogalan
summary: Halfling god of earth, death, and the protection of the dead, the solemn guardian of halfling burial grounds.
content:
## Urogalan

**Title:** He Who Must Be, Lord in the Earth, the Black Hound
**Alignment:** Lawful Neutral
**Domains:** Death, Knowledge
**Symbol:** A silhouette of a dog's head
**Pantheon:** Yondalla's Children

Urogalan is the halfling god of earth, death, and protection of the dead. He is the most somber deity among Yondalla's Children, a quiet, grave figure who watches over halfling burial grounds and guides the spirits of the departed to their final rest. Often depicted as a dark-furred halfling accompanied by a great black hound, Urogalan represents the one aspect of existence that even the most cheerful halfling must eventually face. He teaches that death is a natural part of life, not to be feared but to be respected.

Urogalan's clergy are gravediggers, funeral directors, and spiritual guides who tend to the dying and honor the dead. They maintain burial grounds, perform funeral rites, and comfort the bereaved. They also serve as protectors against undead, which Urogalan considers abominations that violate the natural order of death. Temples to Urogalan are quiet, well-maintained cemeteries and underground crypts where halfling dead are laid to rest with care and dignity. His followers believe that every soul deserves a peaceful passage and that the living honor the dead by remembering them with love.

metadata_json.symbol: A silhouette of a dog's head

title: Baervan Wildwanderer
summary: Gnome god of forests, travel, and nature, the gentle wanderer who roams the woodlands with his raccoon companion.
content:
## Baervan Wildwanderer

**Title:** The Masked Leaf, Father of Fish and Fungus, the Forest Gnome
**Alignment:** Neutral Good
**Domains:** Nature
**Symbol:** A raccoon's face
**Pantheon:** Lords of the Golden Hills

Baervan Wildwanderer is the gnome god of forests, travel, and nature. He is the patron of forest gnomes and all gnomes who feel the call of the wild places, wandering the woodlands of the Outer Planes with his constant companion, a giant raccoon named Chiktikka Fastpaws. Gentle, curious, and endlessly fascinated by the natural world, Baervan delights in discovering new species of plants, befriending woodland creatures, and exploring uncharted forests.

Baervan's clergy are druids, rangers, and naturalists who serve as caretakers of the forests near gnome settlements. They plant trees, tend to sick animals, guide lost travelers through the woods, and protect natural habitats from despoilation. Temples to Baervan are hollowed-out trees, moss-covered clearings, or cozy forest burrows that blend seamlessly with the surrounding woodland. His followers believe that the forest is a living community deserving of respect, and they practice a gentle stewardship that allows gnomes and nature to thrive together in harmony.

title: Baravar Cloakshadow
summary: Gnome god of illusion, deception, and protection, the sly trickster who shields gnomes behind veils of magic.
content:
## Baravar Cloakshadow

**Title:** The Sly One, Master of Illusion, Lord in Disguise
**Alignment:** Neutral Good
**Domains:** Trickery
**Symbol:** A dagger against a cloak
**Pantheon:** Lords of the Golden Hills

Baravar Cloakshadow is the gnome god of illusion, deception, traps, and protection through misdirection. He is the consummate trickster of the gnomish pantheon, using guile, illusions, and cleverness rather than brute force to protect gnome communities from their many enemies. Baravar created many of the magical defenses and illusions that keep gnome warrens hidden from hostile eyes, and he teaches that a well-crafted illusion is worth more than a thousand swords.

Baravar's clergy are illusionists, spies, and trap-makers who specialize in the magical defense of gnome settlements. They weave elaborate illusions to conceal burrow entrances, create traps to confound intruders, and gather intelligence on potential threats through disguise and subterfuge. Temples to Baravar are hidden behind illusions and magical traps, visible only to those who know the proper passwords or can see through his deceptions. His followers believe that the best defense is never being found in the first place, and that cleverness will always triumph over strength.

title: Callarduran Smoothhands
summary: Gnome god of mining, stone, and the deep gnomes, the quiet guardian of the svirfneblin who dwell in the Underdark.
content:
## Callarduran Smoothhands

**Title:** Deep Brother, Master of Stone, Lord of Deepearth
**Alignment:** Neutral
**Domains:** Knowledge, Nature
**Symbol:** A golden signet ring with a ruby star
**Pantheon:** Lords of the Golden Hills

Callarduran Smoothhands is the gnome god of mining, stone carving, and the deep gnomes known as svirfneblin. He is the patron deity of all gnomes who dwell beneath the earth, having led the svirfneblin into the Underdark in ages past and taught them to survive in its hostile depths. Quiet, patient, and enduring as the stone he shapes, Callarduran is more serious than most gnome deities, reflecting the harsh realities faced by his followers in the deep places of the world.

Callarduran's clergy are miners, stone carvers, and spiritual leaders of svirfneblin communities. They locate mineral deposits through prayer and intuition, carve living spaces from solid rock, and maintain the defensive wards that protect deep gnome cities from the many dangers of the Underdark. Temples to Callarduran are magnificent caverns carved into geometric perfection, their walls polished to a mirror shine that reflects the faintest light. His followers believe that the earth is a living body and that those who work the stone with respect and skill will always find shelter and sustenance within it.

metadata_json.symbol: A golden signet ring with a ruby star

tags: deity,god,nonhuman,gnome,lords of the golden hills,mining,stone,svirfneblin,deep gnome,knowledge,nature

title: Flandal Steelskin
summary: Gnome god of mining, smithing, and metalwork, the master craftsman whose forge fires never go cold.
content:
## Flandal Steelskin

**Title:** Master of Metal, the Great Steelsmith, Lord of Smiths
**Alignment:** Neutral Good
**Domains:** Knowledge
**Symbol:** A flaming hammer
**Pantheon:** Lords of the Golden Hills

Flandal Steelskin is the gnome god of mining, physical fitness, smithing, and metalworking. He is the strongest and most physically imposing of the gnome deities, with skin said to be as hard as mithral and a forge that burns with the heat of a star. Flandal teaches that the finest weapons, armor, and tools are created through a combination of physical strength, technical skill, and creative inspiration, and that the act of shaping metal is a form of art as elevated as any other.

Flandal's clergy are smiths, armorers, weaponsmiths, and miners who dedicate their craft to their god. They operate forges in every gnome settlement of significant size, producing tools, weapons, and intricate metalwork of extraordinary quality. Temples to Flandal are built around great forges where the sacred fires burn perpetually, and where master smiths train apprentices in the divine art of metalworking. His followers believe that honest labor at the anvil brings one closer to the divine, and that a perfectly forged blade is a prayer made manifest in steel.

title: Gaerdal Ironhand
summary: Gnome god of vigilance, combat, and protection, the stern warrior who guards gnome communities with iron resolve.
content:
## Gaerdal Ironhand

**Title:** The Stern, Shield of the Golden Hills, the Resolute
**Alignment:** Lawful Good
**Domains:** War
**Symbol:** An iron band
**Pantheon:** Lords of the Golden Hills

Gaerdal Ironhand is the gnome god of vigilance, combat, and martial defense. He is the most serious and humorless of the gnome deities, a stern warrior who has no patience for pranks, tricks, or frivolity when the safety of gnome communities is at stake. Gaerdal is the shield that stands between gnomekind and the many races that would prey upon them, and he demands that his followers maintain constant readiness against attack.

Gaerdal's clergy are warriors, guards, and military commanders who organize the defense of gnome settlements. They train militias, maintain fortifications, patrol borders, and stand ready to repel invaders at a moment's notice. Temples to Gaerdal are fortified strongpoints within gnome warrens, equipped with armories, training grounds, and defensive positions. His followers are respected for their dedication but sometimes viewed as overly stern by their more lighthearted kin. Gaerdal tolerates the antics of other gnome deities because he knows that the freedom to laugh and play is precisely what he fights to protect.

title: Nebelun
summary: Gnome god of invention and luck, the mad tinkerer whose brilliant contraptions work as often by chance as by design.
content:
## Nebelun

**Title:** The Meddler, the Fine Fingers, the Lord of Invention
**Alignment:** Chaotic Good
**Domains:** Knowledge, Trickery
**Symbol:** A pair of bellows and a lizard tail
**Pantheon:** Lords of the Golden Hills

Nebelun is the gnome god of invention, luck, and creative experimentation. He is the patron of tinker gnomes and all gnomes who build improbable contraptions, pursue outlandish theories, and refuse to accept that something cannot be done. Nebelun is perpetually surrounded by a cloud of sparks, gears, and half-finished inventions, and his divine workshop is an ever-expanding labyrinth of machines, devices, and experiments in various states of completion. His creations work as often by sheer luck as by sound engineering principles.

Nebelun's clergy are inventors, tinkerers, and engineers who push the boundaries of what is mechanically possible. They build clockwork devices, experimental weapons, flying machines, and other contraptions that range from the brilliant to the catastrophically unstable. Temples to Nebelun are workshops and laboratories filled with tools, spare parts, and prototype inventions, where the sound of hammering and the occasional explosion are constant companions. His followers believe that every failure is a lesson, every explosion is progress, and that the next invention is always the one that will change the world.

metadata_json.symbol: A pair of bellows and a lizard tail

title: Segojan Earthcaller
summary: Gnome god of earth, nature, and the dead, the gentle caretaker who tends the soil and guides gnome spirits home.
content:
## Segojan Earthcaller

**Title:** Earthfriend, Lord of the Burrow, the Rock Gnome
**Alignment:** Neutral Good
**Domains:** Light
**Symbol:** A glowing gemstone
**Pantheon:** Lords of the Golden Hills

Segojan Earthcaller is the gnome god of earth, nature, and the dead. He is the patron of rock gnomes and burrowing animals, a gentle and nurturing deity who tends to the living earth with the care of a master gardener. Segojan can speak with any creature that burrows through the earth, and he teaches his followers to live in harmony with the underground world. He also serves as the guide for gnome spirits, leading them gently through the earth to their final resting place in the Golden Hills.

Segojan's clergy are druids, herbalists, and caretakers who maintain the delicate balance between gnome communities and the subterranean ecosystems they inhabit. They cultivate underground gardens, tend to burrowing animals, and perform funeral rites that return the dead to the embrace of the earth. Temples to Segojan are underground gardens where bioluminescent fungi and crystals create a soft, warm glow, and where the faithful commune with the spirits of the earth. His followers believe that the earth is generous to those who treat it with respect, and that death is simply a return to the nurturing soil from which all life springs.

title: Urdlen
summary: Gnome god of greed, bloodlust, and evil, the crawling darkness that embodies the worst impulses of gnomekind.
content:
## Urdlen

**Title:** The Crawler Below, the Evil One, the Great Devourer
**Alignment:** Chaotic Evil
**Domains:** Death, War
**Symbol:** A white-clawed mole emerging from the ground
**Pantheon:** Lords of the Golden Hills

Urdlen is the gnome god of greed, bloodlust, spitefulness, uncontrolled impulse, and evil. He is the dark shadow of gnomish nature, representing the capacity for cruelty, avarice, and destruction that lurks beneath the surface of even the most cheerful gnome. Depicted as a massive, blind, white-furred mole with iron claws, Urdlen tunnels through the earth with insatiable hunger, consuming gems, precious metals, and the roots of living things. He is shunned and feared by all right-thinking gnomes.

Urdlen's worship is conducted in secret by gnomes who have given in to their darkest urges. His clergy are murderers, thieves, and saboteurs who work to undermine gnome communities from within, hoarding treasures, poisoning relationships, and committing acts of senseless destruction. Temples to Urdlen are hidden deep underground, their walls scarred by enormous claw marks, and their altars stained with blood and surrounded by stolen gems. His followers believe that the world exists to be consumed, that kindness is weakness, and that the only true power comes from taking what others have and destroying what cannot be taken.

metadata_json.symbol: A white-clawed mole emerging from the ground

title: Bahgtru
summary: Orc god of pure strength and loyalty, the dim-witted but unstoppable son of Gruumsh who crushes all in his path.
content:
## Bahgtru

**Title:** The Leg Breaker, the Strong, Son of Gruumsh
**Alignment:** Chaotic Evil
**Domains:** War
**Symbol:** A broken thigh bone
**Pantheon:** Orc Pantheon

Bahgtru is the orc god of pure brute strength and blind loyalty. He is the son of Gruumsh and the simplest of the orc deities, caring for nothing beyond raw physical power and unquestioning obedience to his father. Bahgtru's strength is legendary even among the gods; he once broke free from the coils of the great serpent of the Abyss through sheer muscle. However, his might is matched only by his stupidity, and he is easily manipulated by cleverer members of the pantheon.

Bahgtru's followers are the strongest warriors in any orc tribe, brutes who lead through physical dominance and crush all opposition with overwhelming force. His clergy encourage contests of strength, train warriors to fight with bare fists and crude weapons, and punish any orc who shows signs of cleverness over might. Temples to Bahgtru are open fighting pits where orcs wrestle, break stones, and prove their worth through feats of strength. His followers believe that might makes right in its purest form, and that the strongest orc is always the one most fit to lead.

title: Ilneval
summary: Orc god of strategy and war, the cunning lieutenant of Gruumsh who plots the conquest of all other races.
content:
## Ilneval

**Title:** The War Maker, the Horde Leader, the Lieutenant of Gruumsh
**Alignment:** Lawful Evil
**Domains:** War
**Symbol:** A blood-red upright sword
**Pantheon:** Orc Pantheon

Ilneval is the orc god of war, strategy, and conquest. He serves as Gruumsh's chief lieutenant and represents the rare orc who combines martial prowess with tactical cunning. Where Gruumsh embodies the savage fury of the orc race, Ilneval channels that fury into disciplined warfare, coordinated assaults, and long-term strategic planning. He is ambitious and calculating, and many believe he secretly plots to one day supplant Gruumsh as ruler of the orc pantheon.

Ilneval's clergy are war chiefs, battle planners, and military advisors who bring organization and strategy to orc warbands. They train orc warriors in formation fighting, siege tactics, and coordinated assaults rather than the chaotic charges that orcs typically favor. Temples to Ilneval are war rooms and command posts decorated with maps, captured battle standards, and the weapons of defeated enemy generals. His followers believe that brute strength without direction is wasted, and that the orc race will only achieve its destiny of conquering all other peoples through disciplined, strategic warfare.

title: Luthic
summary: Orc goddess of fertility, caves, and orc females, the fierce cave mother who nurtures the next generation of warriors.
content:
## Luthic

**Title:** The Cave Mother, the Blood Moon Witch, Mate of Gruumsh
**Alignment:** Neutral Evil
**Domains:** Life, Nature
**Symbol:** An orcish rune meaning "home"
**Pantheon:** Orc Pantheon

Luthic is the orc goddess of fertility, caves, orc females, and the home. She is the mate of Gruumsh and the mother figure of the orc pantheon, embodying the fierce, protective nature of an orc mother defending her brood. Luthic is no gentle nurturer; she is a savage cave bear who tears apart any threat to her children with claws of unbreakable adamantine. She represents the caves and lairs that shelter orc tribes, and the dark wisdom passed down through generations of orc females.

Luthic's clergy are exclusively female, serving as healers, midwives, herbalists, and keepers of the home caves. They tend to the wounded, raise the young, maintain the tribal lair, and preserve the oral traditions and practical knowledge that keep orc tribes alive between battles. Temples to Luthic are the deepest, most secure chambers of orc lairs, where the young are raised and the injured are tended. Her followers believe that the strength of a tribe rests not only in its warriors but in the mothers who birth them, heal them, and keep the home fires burning against the darkness.

title: Shargaas
summary: Orc god of night, stealth, and undeath, the lord of the skulking darkness who commands orc assassins and shadows.
content:
## Shargaas

**Title:** The Night Lord, the Blade in the Darkness, He Who Stalks
**Alignment:** Chaotic Evil
**Domains:** Trickery, Death
**Symbol:** A red crescent moon with a skull between the moon's horns
**Pantheon:** Orc Pantheon

Shargaas is the orc god of darkness, night, stealth, thieves, and the undead. He rules the lightless depths beneath the earth and the darkest hours of the night, commanding those orcs who prefer to kill from the shadows rather than charge screaming into battle. Shargaas is feared and reviled even by other orc deities, for he embodies a form of warfare that most orcs consider dishonorable. Yet his methods are undeniably effective, and even Gruumsh grudgingly acknowledges the utility of assassins and spies.

Shargaas's clergy are assassins, thieves, spies, and necromancers who operate in the shadows of orc society. They carry out covert killings, steal intelligence from enemy camps, and raise undead servants to bolster orc forces. Temples to Shargaas are hidden in the deepest, darkest caverns, where no light has ever penetrated, and where the undead serve as silent sentinels. His followers train in absolute darkness, learning to fight, steal, and kill without sight. They believe that the night belongs to the orcs, and that those who cannot see in the dark deserve whatever comes for them.

metadata_json.symbol: A red crescent moon with a skull

title: Yurtrus
summary: Orc god of death and disease, the white-handed lord of plague whose silent touch brings rotting sickness.
content:
## Yurtrus

**Title:** White Hands, the Lord of Maggots, the Rotting One
**Alignment:** Neutral Evil
**Domains:** Death
**Symbol:** A pair of white hands, palms outward
**Pantheon:** Orc Pantheon

Yurtrus is the orc god of death and disease. He is the most feared and loathed member of the orc pantheon, a silent, rotting figure whose very touch brings plague, pestilence, and decay. Yurtrus never speaks; his will is communicated through visions of rot, the spread of disease, and the movements of vermin. His white hands, covered in a deadly fungal growth, are his most recognizable feature, and orcs invoke his name only in whispered curses or desperate pleas to be spared from sickness.

Yurtrus's clergy are plague priests and death speakers who serve as the orc equivalent of undertakers and disease wardens. They handle the dead, tend to the diseased, and perform dark rituals to appease their god so that he turns his plagues against the enemies of the tribe rather than the tribe itself. Temples to Yurtrus are charnel pits and plague houses, reeking of death and decay, where the diseased are isolated and the dead are disposed of. His followers believe that death and disease are inevitable forces that cannot be fought, only directed, and that those who serve Yurtrus faithfully may choose who suffers and who is spared.

metadata_json.symbol: A pair of white hands, palms outward

title: Maglubiyet
summary: God of goblins, hobgoblins, and bugbears, the iron-fisted tyrant who demands absolute obedience and endless war.
content:
## Maglubiyet

**Title:** The Mighty One, the High Chieftain, the Battle Lord, He Who Rules
**Alignment:** Lawful Evil
**Domains:** War
**Symbol:** A bloody axe
**Pantheon:** Goblinoid Pantheon

Maglubiyet is the chief deity of the goblinoids, ruling over goblins, hobgoblins, and bugbears with an iron fist. He is a brutal and tyrannical god who conquered the original goblinoid deities through sheer force and claimed their worshippers as his own. Maglubiyet demands absolute obedience, endless warfare, and total submission from his followers. He drives the goblinoid races to wage perpetual war against all other peoples, viewing every other race as an enemy to be conquered or enslaved.

Maglubiyet's clergy are war priests and tribal chieftains who rule through fear, force, and the promise of glory in battle. They organize goblinoid hordes for war, enforce discipline through brutal punishment, and conduct blood sacrifices to fuel their god's insatiable hunger for conquest. Temples to Maglubiyet are crude war shrines decorated with the skulls of enemies, where goblinoid warriors are blessed before battle and where the spoils of war are offered to the Mighty One. His followers believe that the goblinoid races are destined to rule the world, and that only through unceasing war and iron discipline can that destiny be fulfilled.

title: Bargrivyek
summary: Goblinoid god of cooperation and territorial dispute resolution, the peacekeeper who prevents goblinoid tribes from destroying each other.
content:
## Bargrivyek

**Title:** The Peacekeeper, the Duty-Bound
**Alignment:** Lawful Evil
**Domains:** War
**Symbol:** A white-tipped flail
**Pantheon:** Goblinoid Pantheon

Bargrivyek is the goblinoid god of cooperation, territory, and the resolution of inter-tribal disputes. He serves as the peacekeeper among goblinoid tribes, enforcing truces and territorial agreements that prevent the various goblinoid races from wasting their strength fighting each other instead of their true enemies. Bargrivyek is not benevolent; his peacekeeping is purely pragmatic, motivated by the understanding that goblinoid hordes are far more dangerous when they work together than when they squabble among themselves.

Bargrivyek's clergy are diplomats, arbiters, and enforcers who negotiate territorial boundaries between goblinoid tribes and punish those who violate agreed-upon truces. They serve as neutral intermediaries between rival chieftains, hammering out treaties and alliances that serve Maglubiyet's greater war effort. Temples to Bargrivyek are neutral meeting grounds where tribal leaders can negotiate without fear of treachery, marked by white banners and protected by strict taboos against violence. His followers believe that goblinoid unity is the key to victory, and that any tribe that breaks a sworn truce deserves swift and brutal punishment.

title: Nomog-Geaya
summary: Hobgoblin god of authority and war, the merciless general who demands iron discipline and military perfection.
content:
## Nomog-Geaya

**Title:** The General, the Taskmaster, Lord of Hobgoblins
**Alignment:** Lawful Evil
**Domains:** War
**Symbol:** A pair of crossed iron handaxes
**Pantheon:** Goblinoid Pantheon

Nomog-Geaya is the hobgoblin god of war, authority, and military discipline. He is the patron of hobgoblin warlords and the embodiment of hobgoblin martial culture, which values rigid hierarchy, tactical precision, and merciless efficiency above all else. Nomog-Geaya demands perfection from his followers, and any hobgoblin who shows weakness, disobedience, or incompetence is swiftly and brutally punished. He serves Maglubiyet loyally but harbors ambitions of elevating the hobgoblins above the other goblinoid races.

Nomog-Geaya's clergy are military officers, drill instructors, and disciplinarians who maintain the iron hierarchy of hobgoblin legions. They enforce strict codes of conduct, train soldiers to fight with mechanical precision, and execute those who fail to meet their exacting standards. Temples to Nomog-Geaya are austere barracks and war colleges where hobgoblin warriors are forged through relentless training and harsh discipline. His followers believe that weakness is a crime, mercy is a luxury for the strong, and that the hobgoblin race will rise to supreme dominance through military superiority.

metadata_json.symbol: A pair of crossed iron handaxes

title: Sardior
summary: God of gem dragons, psionics, and secrets, the Ruby Dragon who holds the balance between Bahamut and Tiamat.
content:
## Sardior

**Title:** The Ruby Dragon, Master of Gem Dragons, the Keeper of Secrets
**Alignment:** Neutral
**Domains:** Knowledge
**Symbol:** A ruby carved in the shape of a dragon's eye
**Pantheon:** Draconic Pantheon

Sardior is the god of gem dragons, psionics, and hidden knowledge. Known as the Ruby Dragon, he was created by Bahamut and Tiamat together in a rare moment of cooperation, meant to serve as a neutral mediator between chromatic and metallic dragonkind. Sardior embodies the balance between good and evil, order and chaos, and his gem dragon children -- amethyst, crystal, emerald, sapphire, and topaz dragons -- share his commitment to neutrality and his fascination with the powers of the mind.

Sardior's influence is felt primarily through the gem dragons and the rare mortal practitioners of psionics who revere him. His clergy, such as it exists, consists of gem dragon elders and psionic adepts who seek to unlock the deepest secrets of the mind and the multiverse. Sardior's followers believe that true power lies not in physical strength or arcane magic, but in the disciplined application of mental energy. The Ruby Dragon watches the eternal conflict between Bahamut and Tiamat from a place of careful neutrality, intervening only when the balance threatens to tip too far in either direction.

title: Io
summary: The Ninefold Dragon, creator of all dragonkind, the primordial dragon god from whom Bahamut and Tiamat were born.
content:
## Io

**Title:** The Ninefold Dragon, the Concordant Dragon, the Great Eternal Wheel, Creator of Dragonkind
**Alignment:** Neutral
**Domains:** Knowledge
**Symbol:** A multi-colored metallic disk
**Pantheon:** Draconic Pantheon

Io is the supreme creator god of all dragonkind, a primordial being of unfathomable power who existed before the division of dragons into chromatic, metallic, and gem varieties. Known as the Ninefold Dragon, Io encompasses all alignments, all elements, and all aspects of draconic nature within his incomprehensible form. According to draconic myth, Io was split in two during a battle with a primordial, and from his sundered halves arose Bahamut and Tiamat, forever embodying the opposing aspects of their progenitor.

Io's worship is rare in the present age, as most dragons direct their devotion toward Bahamut, Tiamat, or Sardior. Those few who still revere the Ninefold Dragon tend to be ancient dragons, dragonborn sages, or scholars who seek to understand the fundamental unity that underlies all dragonkind. Io represents totality, the idea that creation and destruction, good and evil, order and chaos are all aspects of a single cosmic whole. His followers seek to transcend the divisions that separate dragonkind, believing that only by reuniting the scattered aspects of Io can the dragons achieve their true potential as the supreme race of the multiverse.

title: Annam All-Father
summary: Supreme god of giants, creator and patriarch of all giantkind, who withdrew from his children in disappointment.
content:
## Annam All-Father

**Title:** The All-Father, the Creator, the Prime, the Great Creator of All Giants
**Alignment:** Neutral
**Domains:** Knowledge, Nature
**Symbol:** A pair of clasped hands
**Pantheon:** Ordning (Giant Pantheon)

Annam All-Father is the creator god and supreme deity of the giants, father of all the giant races and architect of the Ordning, the strict social hierarchy that governs giant society. In ages past, Annam walked among his children and guided them directly, but he eventually withdrew from the world in disappointment at the constant warfare between giant races and their failure to live up to his vision of a unified giant civilization that would rival the gods themselves.

Annam's influence is still felt through the Ordning and the cultural traditions he established, even though he no longer answers prayers or grants spells directly. His legacy shapes every aspect of giant society, from the storm giants who sit atop the Ordning to the hill giants who languish at its bottom. Some giant sages and priests still seek to win back Annam's favor by uniting the giant races or achieving great deeds worthy of his attention. They believe that if the giants could overcome their divisions and fulfill Annam's original vision, the All-Father would return and lead them to their rightful place as rulers of the world.

title: Stronmaus
summary: God of storm giants, sun, and weather, the joyful sky lord who hurls thunderbolts and revels in the tempest.
content:
## Stronmaus

**Title:** The Storm Lord, the Smiling God, Lord of Storm Giants
**Alignment:** Neutral Good
**Domains:** Tempest
**Symbol:** A forked lightning bolt descending from a cloud
**Pantheon:** Ordning (Giant Pantheon)

Stronmaus is the god of storm giants, the sun, sky, weather, and joy. He is the eldest and most favored son of Annam All-Father, and he serves as the de facto leader of the giant pantheon in his father's absence. Unlike many giant deities who are grim and warlike, Stronmaus is exuberant and joyful, delighting in the raw power of thunderstorms, the warmth of the sun, and the beauty of the sky. His laughter rolls across the heavens like thunder, and his tears fall as warm summer rain.

Stronmaus's clergy are storm giant priests and weather-workers who command the power of storms and serve as leaders and advisors among giant communities. They read omens in lightning patterns, call down rain for parched lands, and unleash devastating tempests against enemies of giantkind. Temples to Stronmaus are built on the highest mountaintops and tallest cloud castles, open to the sky so that the faithful can feel the wind and rain on their faces. His followers believe that the storms are a gift from their god, both a display of his power and a reminder of the joy that comes from embracing the wild forces of nature.

metadata_json.symbol: A forked lightning bolt descending from a cloud

title: Skoraeus Stonebones
summary: God of stone giants, art, and the earth, the contemplative sculptor who shaped the first mountains.
content:
## Skoraeus Stonebones

**Title:** King of the Rock, the Living Rock, the Great Creator
**Alignment:** Neutral
**Domains:** Knowledge
**Symbol:** A stalactite
**Pantheon:** Ordning (Giant Pantheon)

Skoraeus Stonebones is the god of stone giants, earth, and art carved from living rock. He is among the wisest and most reclusive of the giant deities, preferring the quiet contemplation of deep caverns to the conflicts of the surface world. Skoraeus shaped the first mountains and taught the stone giants to see the beauty hidden within raw stone, revealing the sculptures and carvings that wait within every boulder and cliff face. His understanding of the earth runs deeper than any other being's.

Skoraeus's clergy are stone giant artists, sages, and earth-speakers who spend centuries perfecting their craft and communing with the living stone. They carve magnificent sculptures in underground galleries, interpret the whispers of the earth to predict earthquakes and volcanic eruptions, and guard ancient secrets sealed within the bedrock of the world. Temples to Skoraeus are vast subterranean galleries filled with stone carvings of breathtaking artistry, where the faithful meditate in silence and seek inspiration from the patterns in the rock. His followers believe that the earth itself is a work of art in progress, and that those who listen carefully can hear the stone singing.

title: Surtur
summary: God of fire giants, craft, and war, the master smith who forges weapons of destruction in his volcanic fortress.
content:
## Surtur

**Title:** Lord of the Fire Giants, the Lord of Muspelheim, the Enemy of the Gods
**Alignment:** Lawful Evil
**Domains:** Knowledge, War
**Symbol:** A flaming sword
**Pantheon:** Ordning (Giant Pantheon)

Surtur is the god of fire giants, fire, craft, and war. He is the most militant and ambitious of the giant deities, a master smith and ruthless warlord who forges devastating weapons in the volcanic depths of his realm, Muspelheim. Surtur's burning ambition is nothing less than the destruction of the existing world order and the establishment of fire giant supremacy over all creation. He wields a massive flaming sword said to be capable of severing the roots of the world tree, and his legions of fire giant warriors stand ever ready for the final war.

Surtur's clergy are fire giant priests, war leaders, and master smiths who combine religious devotion with military discipline and craft mastery. They forge weapons and armor of exceptional quality in volcanic forges, train fire giant armies for conquest, and plot the subjugation of all other races. Temples to Surtur are built within active volcanoes and great forges, where rivers of lava provide both illumination and raw material. His followers believe that the world will end in fire, and that when the flames finally consume all, the fire giants will emerge from the ashes to rule whatever remains.

title: Thrym
summary: God of frost giants, cold, and brute strength, the ice king who rules through terror and raw physical power.
content:
## Thrym

**Title:** Lord of the Frost Giants, the Lord of Blizzards, the Ice King
**Alignment:** Chaotic Evil
**Domains:** War
**Symbol:** A double-bladed white greataxe
**Pantheon:** Ordning (Giant Pantheon)

Thrym is the god of frost giants, cold, ice, and brute strength. He is the ruler of the frost giant race, a towering figure of savage power who embodies the merciless cold of the frozen wastelands his people call home. Thrym values raw strength and cruelty above all else, and his rule is maintained through intimidation, violence, and the constant threat of destruction. He wages eternal war against the forces of warmth and civilization, seeking to cover the world in an endless winter.

Thrym's clergy are frost giant chieftains, shamans, and berserkers who lead their people through strength and savagery. They conduct raids against warmer lands, offer sacrifices of captured prisoners to appease the cold, and maintain the brutal hierarchy of frost giant society through constant challenges and displays of dominance. Temples to Thrym are glacial caverns and ice fortresses, their walls carved with scenes of conquest and decorated with the frozen trophies of defeated enemies. His followers believe that the cold is the natural state of the world, and that the warmth of civilization is a temporary aberration that will inevitably be scoured away by ice and wind.

title: Grolantor
summary: God of hill giants, hunger, and war, the gluttonous brute who devours everything in his path.
content:
## Grolantor

**Title:** The Glutton, He of the Wide Mouth, Lord of Hill Giants
**Alignment:** Chaotic Evil
**Domains:** War
**Symbol:** A wooden club
**Pantheon:** Ordning (Giant Pantheon)

Grolantor is the god of hill giants, gluttony, hunger, and war. He is the least of Annam's sons, a dim-witted, perpetually ravenous brute whose insatiable appetite mirrors that of the hill giants who worship him. Grolantor thinks of nothing beyond his next meal and his next fight, and his followers emulate this simple philosophy with enthusiastic brutality. Cast out by the other giant gods for his stupidity and voracious appetites, Grolantor embodies the degradation that befalls those who abandon higher purpose in favor of base appetite.

Grolantor's clergy are the biggest and meanest hill giants in any tribe, leading through intimidation and the ability to eat more than anyone else. They organize raids on farms, villages, and caravans to feed the tribe's endless hunger, and they conduct crude rituals that involve the consumption of vast quantities of food and drink. Temples to Grolantor are little more than feasting halls and food stores, piled high with stolen provisions and the bones of previous meals. His followers believe that might makes right, that the strong eat and the weak are eaten, and that the purpose of every living thing is ultimately to serve as food for something bigger and hungrier.
